reproducible_artifact: A 'three-checkpoint identity check' practice for senior engineers and engineering teams. Identify the three checkpoints in your week where outcome-pairs land: (1) the dashboard / telemetry checkpoint (sukha/duḥkha analog), (2) the launch-vs-target measurement (lābha/alābha analog), (3) the competitive-position checkpoint (jaya/ajaya analog). Build the practice of arriving at each as the same engineer regardless of which way each one resolved. Read all three accurately. Do not let any of the three rewrite who you are. From that state, engage in the next deploy / decision / review.
verdict_reason: The engineering layer's claim is reducible to a concrete weekly practice. The three checkpoints are role-anchored and tool-specific (Datadog, retrospective, competitor demo). The implication field directly states the artifact ('Practice arriving at the dashboard, the retrospective, and the competitor's release as the same engineer regardless of which way each one moved'). Reproducible across teams: any engineering organization has the three pair-registers.
